statusLine: EU fuel reserves are under strain with 19 of 27 countries below mandatory minimums in at least one fuel type, while oil prices have surged 51% since late February.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security is under significant pressure as supply disruptions from Middle East conflicts continue to bite. Despite EU-wide averages appearing adequate (petrol 103.8 days, diesel 96.2 days), the reality is far more concerning at the country level. Romania faces the most severe shortages with all three fuel types in critical status - petrol at just 33.2 days and diesel at 32.4 days, both well below the mandatory 90-day minimum. Poland and Luxembourg also show critical levels across all fuel categories.

The impact varies dramatically by fuel type. Diesel reserves are particularly strained, with 15 of 27 countries below the 90-day threshold, including major economies like Germany (64.5 days), France (83.4 days), and Italy (64.3 days). Jet fuel presents the most acute challenge, with 19 countries in critical status, reflecting the aviation sector's vulnerability to supply disruptions.

Oil prices underscore the severity of the crisis, with Brent crude jumping from $72.48 to $109.3 per barrel since late February - a 51% increase that's feeding through to pump prices. The Netherlands now faces the EU's highest petrol costs at €2.324 per liter, while regional disparities create additional market pressures. Countries like Finland maintain strong diesel reserves (187.4 days) but struggle with jet fuel (40.2 days), highlighting the complex, fuel-specific nature of current shortages.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries have at least one fuel type below the mandatory 90-day minimum reserves
Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg face critical shortages across all three fuel types
Diesel reserves are below minimum in 15 countries, including major economies Germany, France, and Italy
Jet fuel shows the most widespread shortages with 19 countries in critical status
Oil prices have surged 51% since late February, reaching $109.3/barrel and driving pump prices to €2.32/L in the Netherlands
